Details:
Chlorine can effectively inactivate V. cholerae. One study reported 100 percent inactivation of V. chlorae in tap water and distilled water with a free chlorine dose of 1.0 mg/L and contact times of 10, 20 and 30 minutes [1666]. Two other studies on point-of-use water treatment products containing chlorine-based disinfectant reported more than 7.5 log V. cholerae inactivation [956, 957].
The CDC recommmends disinfection with 30 min contact with 2 drops of household bleach in a liter of water [2008].
